pr.title: Tests: skip symlink-escape safety tests when Windows symlink privilege is unavailable (WinError 1314)
pr.description:
## Summary

Lane-2 Windows validation hit `tests/test_capability_confidence.py::TestSafety::test_symlink_escape_rejected` failing with `OSError: [WinError 1314] A required privilege is not held by the client` — a test-harness environment limitation (Windows needs a privilege or Developer Mode to create symlinks), not an Axiom runtime failure.

The identical `link.symlink_to(target)` pattern exists in **42 symlink-escape safety tests** across the suite (frameworks, registries, configs, coding trials), all of which would fail the same way on a Windows full-suite run. Rather than patching one, this adds a shared helper and applies it everywhere:

```python
# tests/conftest.py
def make_symlink_or_skip(link: Path, target) -> None:
    try:
        link.symlink_to(target)
    except OSError as exc:
        pytest.skip(f"symlink creation unavailable: {exc}")
```

Every call site becomes `make_symlink_or_skip(link, target)`. Where symlink creation works (Linux/macOS/CI, Windows with the privilege) the escape scenario is constructed and the full safety assertion runs unchanged; where it can't be constructed the test skips with the OS error in the skip reason. **No production path-safety logic is touched** — only test scaffolding.

Also appends the lane-2-confirmed note to the BHV-037 ledger entry: the one-time `very_high (1.0) → medium (0.5)` published-confidence drop on the first accepted application after #63 is **expected doctrine** (old undamped reports are historical evidence; the new report is computed under the damped rule), not a regression.

Tests: full pytest 5175 passed locally (excl. atlas/local-runner suites, unaffected by this change; CI runs everything); ruff clean on tests/.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Tests: skip symlink-escape safety tests when symlink creation is unavailable

Windows requires a privilege (or Developer Mode) to create symlinks
(WinError 1314); without it the symlink-escape scenario cannot be
constructed, so the safety tests skip instead of failing. Shared
make_symlink_or_skip helper in tests/conftest.py applied to all 42
symlink-escape call sites; production path-safety logic untouched.
Also records the expected one-time first-post-damping confidence
re-projection in the BHV-037 ledger entry (lane-2 confirmed).

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
